User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the -MIZUIRO- Rakuten Auto Afi application, storing their profile, subscription status, and integration credentials.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The email address associated with the user's account, primarily from Firebase Authentication.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

paymentStatus.description: The current payment status of the user's subscription (e.g., 'active', 'trialing', 'canceled', 'unpaid').

stripeCustomerId.description: Reference to the customer ID in Stripe for managing subscriptions and payments. (Relationship: User 1:1 StripeCustomer)

rakutenApplicationId.description: The Application ID provided by the user to access the Rakuten Affiliate API on their behalf. Stored securely.

rakutenAffiliateId.description: The Affiliate ID provided by the user to use the Rakuten Affiliate API. Stored securely.

googleApiKey.description: The Google API Key provided by the user for accessing Google AI services (e.g., Gemini).

pinterestAccessToken.description: OAuth 2.0 access token for Pinterest API integration, enabling automated posting. Stored securely.

pinterestRefreshToken.description: OAuth 2.0 refresh token for Pinterest API integration, used to obtain new access tokens. Stored securely.

pinterestExpiresAt.description: Timestamp when the Pinterest access token expires.

threadsAccessToken.description: OAuth 2.0 access token for Threads API integration, enabling automated posting. Stored securely.

threadsRefreshToken.description: OAuth 2.0 refresh token for Threads API integration, used to obtain new access tokens. Stored securely.

threadsExpiresAt.description: Timestamp when the Threads access token expires.

pinterestUserId.description: The unique identifier of the Pinterest account linked by the user.

threadsUserId.description: The unique identifier of the Threads account linked by the user.

averageRakutenUnitValue.description: A user-defined average unit value for Rakuten affiliate products, used to calculate estimated revenue.

Persona.title: Persona
Persona.description: Defines an AI-driven character with specific attributes (tone, image style, target audience) used for generating diverse social media content.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Persona entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this persona. (Relationship: User 1:N Persona)

name.description: A user-friendly name for the persona (e.g., 'Tech Guru', 'Lifestyle Blogger').

tone.description: The specific tone of voice for content generated by this persona (e.g., 'friendly', 'formal', 'humorous').

imageStyle.description: The preferred visual style for images generated for this persona (e.g., 'minimalist', 'photorealistic', 'cartoonish').

targetAudience.description: A description of the target audience this persona aims to attract (e.g., 'tech enthusiasts', 'young mothers', 'DIY hobbyists').

description.description: An optional detailed description or notes about the persona.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the persona was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the persona was last updated.

Post.title: Post
Post.description: Represents a piece of social media content (generated by AI), including its text, prompts, and status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Post entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this post.

topic.description: The original topic or product name used for generation.

postText.description: The generated textual content of the social media post.

imagePrompt.description: The prompt for generating an accompanying image.

persona.description: A snapshot of the persona object used for generation.

rakutenProduct.description: A snapshot of the selected Rakuten product details.

status.description: The current status of the post (e.g., 'draft', 'scheduled', 'posted').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the post was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the post was last updated.

AnalyticsDashboard.title: AnalyticsDashboard
AnalyticsDashboard.description: Stores aggregated performance metrics for a specific user's dashboard, derived from post activities and click tracking.

userId.description: Unique identifier for the User entity, serving as the primary key for this 1:1 relationship with User.

totalClicks.description: The total number of clicks recorded for all shortened affiliate URLs associated with the user.

estimatedRevenue.description: The estimated revenue calculated based on total clicks and the user's average Rakuten unit value setting.

lastUpdatedAt.description: Timestamp when these aggregated analytics were last updated.

PostHistory.title: PostHistory
PostHistory.description: Logs individual events and outcomes related to social media post operations, such as generation, scheduling, publishing, and failures.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PostHistory entry.

userId.description: Reference to the User associated with this history entry. (Relationship: User 1:N PostHistory)

postId.description: Reference to the Post entity this history entry pertains to. (Relationship: Post 1:N PostHistory)

timestamp.description: The exact time when the operation occurred.

success.description: Indicates whether the operation was successful (true) or failed (false).

details.description: A detailed message or JSON string containing additional information about the operation, such as error messages or success parameters.

Announcement.title: Announcement
Announcement.description: Stores administrative announcements to be displayed on user dashboards.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Announcement entity.

content.description: The main body content of the announcement.

publishDate.description: The date and time when the announcement was published.

isGlobal.description: Indicates if the announcement is visible to all users (true) or specific users (false). Assumed to be true for general announcements to 'user dashboards'.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the announcement record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the announcement record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores user profile information, payment status, Rakuten API credentials, and social media OAuth tokens. Owned privately by the authenticated user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Defines AI-driven characters for content generation, owned by a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns this persona.

params.name: personaId
params.description: The unique ID of the persona.

definition.description: Represents social media content generated for publishing. Owned by a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'personaId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns this post.

params.name: postId
params.description: The unique ID of the post.

definition.description: Logs individual events and outcomes related to social media post operations for a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'postId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user associated with this history entry.

params.name: historyId
params.description: The unique ID of the post history entry.

definition.description: Stores aggregated performance metrics for a specific user's dashboard. The document ID is the 'userId' for direct, authorized access.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user to whom this analytics dashboard belongs. This is also the document ID.

definition.description: Stores administrative announcements visible to all authenticated users. Only administrators can create or update these documents.

params.name: announcementId
params.description: The unique ID of the announcement.

definition.description: A collection used for Database Access Control (DBAC) to define administrator roles. The existence of a document at this path for a given 'userId' grants administrative privileges. Document content can be minimal.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who has administrative privileges. This is the document ID.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for -MIZUIRO- Rakuten Auto Afi is designed around core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and clear Access Modeling to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability. All user-specific data is organized hierarchically under a `/users/{userId}` top-level collection, with the exception of the `AnalyticsDashboard` which resides in a dedicated top-level collection, `analytics_dashboards/{userId}`, where the document ID directly corresponds to the `userId`. This ensures a single, easily discoverable document for each user's aggregated analytics.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** This design explicitly prioritizes avoiding `get()` calls in security rules. For user-owned subcollections (e.g., `personas`, `posts`, `postHistory`), the parent `userId` is always included as a field within each document. For instance, a `Persona` document stored at `/users/{userId}/personas/{personaId}` will contain a `userId` field. This denormalization allows security rules to evaluate `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` directly, without needing to perform a `get()` operation on a parent document. This approach makes rules atomic, improves performance, and prevents complex debugging issues related to hierarchical dependencies. The `AnalyticsDashboard` utilizes `userId` as its document ID in the `analytics_dashboards` collection, inherently linking it to the authenticated user without any `get()`.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** Secure `list` operations are supported through structural segregation and explicit ownership. All user-specific data is nested under `/users/{userId}` (or directly identified by `userId` in `analytics_dashboards`). This path-based ownership means that any `list` query issued by a client must include `request.auth.uid` as the `{userId}` wildcard in the path. Firestore security rules can then enforce `allow list: if request.auth.uid == userId`, ensuring that users can only list documents belonging to themselves. Global `announcements` are in a top-level collection, `/announcements/{announcementId}`, allowing all authenticated users to read without complex filtering. Administrative roles are managed via an `existence` check in the `/roles_admin/{userId}` collection, which is also an atomic and performant way to implement DBAC without filtering concerns.

**Structural Segregation:** Data is segregated based on its security posture. For example, all `Post` documents, regardless of their `status` (draft, scheduled, posted), belong to a single user and are stored under `/users/{userId}/posts`. The `status` field then dictates *what actions* can be performed on the document by its owner, rather than defining different access permissions for different user groups, thus maintaining a homogeneous security posture within the collection. Global announcements are separate from user-specific data, and admin roles have their own dedicated collection. This simplifies rule logic significantly by moving complexity from rule conditions into the data structure itself.
